textPlain: Yes, simpler times and such. And I get the feeling someone is about to discover RFC 864, which is even more fun (as in: a DDOS amplification vector of note, but this stuff actually was useful for a while...)
The networked webscale database we've all been waiting for.

https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc864.html> UDP Based Character Generator Service> When a datagram is received, an answering datagram is sent
   containing a random number (between 0 and 512) of characters (the
   data in the received datagram is ignored).> The service only send one datagram in response to each received
   datagram, so there is no concern about the service sending data
   faster than the user can process it.Oof...Yeah apparently the idea that the "user" might not be the real sender wasn't yet well-known.Simpler times indeed.
